title: Quantum computing: Tracking qubit fluctuations in real time - Digital Journal
why:
- Researchers have developed a real-time monitoring system that tracks qubit fluctuations about 100 times faster than previous methods.
- The system uses fast FPGA-based control hardware to identify instantly when a qubit shifts from “good” to “bad,” enabling rapid response to qubit instability.
- Standard testing methods previously took up to a minute to measure qubit performance, missing rapid fluctuations and only providing average values.
- The new method updates its estimate of a qubit’s relaxation (energy loss) rate within milliseconds, matching the natural speed of qubit fluctuations.
- By continually refining its measurements in real-time, this advance opens a new path toward stabilizing and scaling future quantum processors.
